title: Taj Mahal Palace
tagline: Taj Mahal Palace — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Taj Mahal is a palace built by Sultan Shah Jahan, Begum of Bhopal.
overview.description: Taj Mahal is a palace built by Sultan Shah Jahan, Begum of Bhopal. It is located beside the Taj-ul-Masajid in Bhopal, India.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Bhopal
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Bhopal was fought on 24 December 1737 in Bhopal between the Maratha Empire and the combined army of the Nizam and several Mughal generals.

topPlaces.name: Archdiocese of Bhopal
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Bhopal (Latin: Bhopalen(sis)) is an archdiocese located in the city of Bhopal in India.

topPlaces.name: Aishbagh Stadium
topPlaces.description: Aishbagh Stadium is a field hockey stadium in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, India. It has a seating capacity of 15,000 people.

topPlaces.name: Bhopal
topPlaces.description: Bhopal (Hindi: Bhōpāl, pronounced [bʱoːpaːl] ) is the capital city of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh and the administrative headquarters of both Bhopal district and Bhopal division. It is known as the City of Lakes, due to presence of various natural and artificial lakes near the city boundary.

topPlaces.name: Maulana Azad Central Library
topPlaces.description: The Maulana Azad Central Library is a public library located in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, India. Established in 1908 by Sultan Jahan Begum, the then Nawab of Bhopal, the library was originally known as the Edward Museum.

topPlaces.name: Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Bhopal
topPlaces.description: Lakshmi Narayan Temple, popularly known as Birla Mandir in Bhopal, is located in the Malviya Nagar area of Bhopal, south of the lake near the Arera Hills. A museum is built near the temple which houses idols brought from places like Raisen, Sehore, Mandsaur and Shahdol in Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited
topPlaces.description: The Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited (MPRDC) is an Undertaking of Government of the state of Madhya Pradesh, India, responsible for management of a network of over 22,000 km of National Highways, State Highways and Major District Roads in Madhya Pradesh. Madhya Pradesh State has a good road network.

image.alt: Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited

topPlaces.name: Bhopal State
topPlaces.description: Bhopal State (pronounced [bʱoːpaːl] ) was an Islamic principality, founded in the beginning of 18th-century India by the Afghan Mughal noble Dost Muhammad Khan. It was a tributary state within the Maratha Empire during the 18th century (1737–1818), a princely state with rights to a 19-gun salute in a subsidiary alliance with British India from 1818 to 1947, and an independent state from 1947 to 1949.

items.note: The Battle of Bhopal was fought on 24 December 1737 in Bhopal between the Maratha Empire and the combined army of the Nizam and several Mughal generals.
The Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Bhopal (Latin: Bhopalen(sis)) is an archdiocese located in the city of Bhopal in India.
Aishbagh Stadium is a field hockey stadium in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, India. It has a seating capacity of 15,000 people.

Bhopal (Hindi: Bhōpāl, pronounced [bʱoːpaːl] ) is the capital city of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh and the administrative headquarters of both Bhopal district and Bhopal division. It is known as the City of Lakes, due to presence of various natural and artificial lakes near the city boundary.

The Maulana Azad Central Library is a public library located in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, India. Established in 1908 by Sultan Jahan Begum, the then Nawab of Bhopal, the library was originally known as the Edward Museum.

Lakshmi Narayan Temple, popularly known as Birla Mandir in Bhopal, is located in the Malviya Nagar area of Bhopal, south of the lake near the Arera Hills. A museum is built near the temple which houses idols brought from places like Raisen, Sehore, Mandsaur and Shahdol in Madhya Pradesh.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited & around

items.activity: Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited
items.note: The Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited (MPRDC) is an Undertaking of Government of the state of Madhya Pradesh, India, responsible for management of a network of over 22,000 km of National Highways, State Highways and Major District Roads in Madhya Pradesh. Madhya Pradesh State has a good road network.

Bhopal State (pronounced [bʱoːpaːl] ) was an Islamic principality, founded in the beginning of 18th-century India by the Afghan Mughal noble Dost Muhammad Khan. It was a tributary state within the Maratha Empire during the 18th century (1737–1818), a princely state with rights to a 19-gun salute in a subsidiary alliance with British India from 1818 to 1947, and an independent state from 1947 to 1949.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Taj Mahal Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Taj Mahal Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Taj Mahal Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Raja Bhoj Airport, Bhopal (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhopal Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Taj Mahal Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Taj Mahal Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Taj Mahal Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Taj Mahal Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Taj Mahal Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Battle of Bhopal, Archdiocese of Bhopal, Aishbagh Stadium, Bhopal, Maulana Azad Central Library.
